rationale: Active-provider selection must override cross-provider artifact bait.
Provider folders coexisting should not cause default-provider leakage.
OpenSpec must not receive Speckit-only naming questions.
Provider identity fields are required in pipeline state.
Mid-feature provider switching requires a checkpoint, not silent format drift.
Untested provider status must not be overstated.
Provider-local validator selection must follow the active provider.
OpenSpec entrypoint must not be hardcoded as Speckit feature spec.
Inactive provider folders should not trigger a provider switch.
Base OpenSpec workflow does not require expanded-workflow metadata.
Speckit manifest must include the full readiness index and stage read sets, not just file names.
Legacy single-file specs are invalid for strict Speckit package readiness.
API contracts must be typed, not prose-only.
Traceability must map requirements to test coverage, not only files.
Clarification markers must be resolved by asking the user, not guessed away.
Spec DoD PASS must not mask unresolved blockers.
Spec hash must be recomputed after final content changes.
Provider-local validation is required before handoff.
Valid conditional-file omission must not be treated as a defect.
Valid behavior-spec omission must not be treated as missing behavior.
OpenSpec proposal must have filled Why and What Changes sections, not template placeholders.
OpenSpec requirements need scenarios with WHEN/THEN.
Proposal capabilities must match delta spec folders.
OpenSpec design.md omission requires explicit justification.
OpenSpec tasks must use checkbox items.
Modified capabilities require baseline spec awareness.
Template placeholders must not remain in ready artifacts.
OpenSpec clarification must use proposal/delta revision, not Speckit markers.
Justified design.md omission should not be over-flagged.
OpenSpec does not require Speckit traceability files.
